task: I’ve got a multiplication quiz coming up, and I need a fast way to get the facts to stick before I take it. Can you help me make a simple practice plan that actually works in a short amount of time?

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s a simple short-time plan that actually helps facts stick:

### 10-minute multiplication practice plan
**1. Pick the hard facts first (2 min)**  
Write down the ones you miss most.

**2. Say them out loud while looking at the answer (2 min)**  
Example: “7 × 8 = 56.”  
Do this a few times for each one.

**3. Cover-and-check drill (3 min)**  
- Look at the fact
- Cover the answer
- Try to say it
- Uncover and check

**4. Quick mixed practice (2 min)**  
Mix easy and hard ones so your brain has to really remember.

**5. Final speed round (1 min)**  
Go through only the hard ones as fast as you can.

### Best rule
Don’t just reread them.  
**Try to recall the answer first** — that’s what makes it stick.

If you want, I can also make you a **super short 5-minute version** or a **practice sheet** with the exact facts you should review.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, but make it more like a real kid plan, not a school poster. 10 minutes is fine. I need the exact order and what to do if I keep messing up the same ones. Also, give me a super short version — like 5 minutes — because sometimes I’m not doing the whole thing.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s a **real kid version**.

## 10-minute multiplication plan
**1. Grab the facts you keep missing — 2 min**  
Write the ones you mess up most.

**2. Say each one out loud — 2 min**  
Example: “7 × 8 = 56.”  
Say it a few times.

**3. Cover it and try first — 3 min**  
- Look at the fact  
- Cover the answer  
- Say it from memory  
- Uncover and check

**4. Do a mixed practice round — 2 min**  
Mix easy ones with hard ones.

**5. End with the same hard ones again — 1 min**  
Go back to the ones you missed.

## If you keep messing up the same ones
Do this:
- **stop and say the fact 3 times**
- **write it once**
- **try it again right away**
- if you still miss it, **circle it and come back later**

## 5-minute version
**1. Pick the hard facts — 1 min**  
**2. Cover-and-say practice — 2 min**  
**3. Do the same missed ones again — 1 min**  
**4. Quick speed round — 1 min**

## Fast rule
**Don’t just look at them. Try to answer first.**

If you want, I can make you a **tiny practice list of the hardest multiplication facts** too.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, better. But I want it more like what I actually do, not just a neat list. Like: first I grab paper, then I do the ones I miss, then I hit the same bad ones again. And for the ones I keep messing up, maybe give me a trick besides just writing it 3 times, because that gets annoying fast. Also make the 5-minute one even more stripped down. Like dumb simple.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s the **actual do-this-next** version.

## 10-minute plan
**1. Grab paper + pencil.**  
**2. Write only the facts you keep missing.**  
**3. Do them once בלי looking.**  
**4. Check your answers.**  
**5. Circle the ones you missed.**  
**6. Do only the circled ones again.**  
**7. If you miss the same one again, say it like a sentence:**  
“7 times 8 is 56.”  
Then **cover it and say it again right away.**  
**8. Finish with one fast round of all the hard ones.**

## If you keep messing up the same fact
Don’t just write it a bunch.

Do this instead:
- **say it out loud**
- **split it up** if it helps:  
  - “7 × 8”  
  - “56”
- **make a tiny trick** for it:
  - **doubles**: 6×6, 7×7
  - **near doubles**: 6×7 = 42, so 6×8 is one more row
  - **skip count** by that number
- **come back to it right away**

## 5-minute dumb-simple version
**1. Grab paper.**  
**2. Do the hard ones.**  
**3. Circle the misses.**  
**4. Do the misses again.**  
**5. Say the worst ones out loud one last time.**

## One rule
**Miss it once = do it again right away.**

If you want, I can turn this into a **super tiny checklist you can follow without thinking**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s closer. But I want it even more like a kid actually doing it, not like steps on a worksheet.

Like, make it sound like:
- grab paper
- do the bad ones first
- circle the ones I miss
- hit those again right away
- if I still miss it, use a trick for that one
- then do a fast last round

And for the trick part, give me a few that are easy to actually use, not just “say it out loud.” Like ones that help when I freeze on the same fact.

The 5-minute one should be almost stupid simple. Just the bare thing I do, not a bunch of words.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s the **kid-doing-it** version.

## 10-minute plan
- **Grab paper and pencil**
- **Do the bad ones first**
- **Circle the ones you miss**
- **Do those same ones again right away**
- **If you miss one again, use a trick**
- **Do one fast last round**

## Easy tricks for the ones you keep freezing on
Use one of these:

- **Double it**
  - 6×6 = 6 + 6
  - 7×7 = 7 + 7

- **One more / one less**
  - If you know 6×7, then 6×8 is just one more group of 6

- **Skip count**
  - Count by 4s, 5s, 6s, 7s, or 8s until you hit it

- **Break it apart**
  - 7×8 = 7×5 + 7×3

- **Tiny memory picture**
  - Pick a silly thing that matches the fact and use that every time

## 5-minute version
- **Grab paper**
- **Do the bad ones**
- **Circle misses**
- **Do the misses again**
- **Done**

If you want, I can make you a **super tiny “before the quiz” cheat sheet** with just the hardest facts to check.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, closer. But that last one is still a little too neat. I want it like a kid actually doing it, not classroom stuff.

Like:
- grab paper
- do the bad ones first
- circle the ones I miss
- do those same ones again right away
- if I miss it again, use a trick for that one
- then do a fast last round

And the trick part, make it actually usable when I blank out. Like not a long lesson. Just a few quick tricks I can grab fast.

5-minute one should be almost nothing. Just the bare moves, no extra talking.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s the **real kid version**.

## 10-minute
- grab paper
- do the bad ones first
- circle the ones you miss
- do those same ones again right away
- if you miss one again, use a trick for that one
- then do a fast last round

## quick tricks for when you blank
- **make a smaller fact you know**
  - if 7×8 freezes you, think: 7×5 = 35, then add 21
- **double then add**
  - 6×7 = double 3×7
- **count up from a fact you know**
  - know 6×6? then 6×7 is just 6 more
- **make a picture**
  - 4 groups of 6 = 4 rows of 6 dots in your head
- **skip that one, come back**
  - if your brain locks up, circle it and move on fast

## 5-minute
- grab paper
- do the bad ones
- circle misses
- do them again
- done

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-line checklist** you can look at right before the quiz.

user_simulator.text: Yeah. That’s it. Short, not all school-y. I can use that.
